title: Lume — Control Room
description: Real-time 30-min settlement window + feed health for on-call operators.

panels.title: Feed Status
panels.description: LIVE when vendor data arrived in last 150 s. DEAD when window:last_batch key expired.

panels.title: Filling Gaps (Backfill)
panels.description: Banner appears when Spark is processing historical backfill payloads. kWh spikes during this time are expected.

panels.title: kWh — Current 30-min Window
panels.description: Running total for the current settlement window. Threshold at 90% of the target (10,800 kWh for 12,000 homes × 0.9 kWh avg).

panels.title: Forecast — End of Window
panels.description: Projected kWh at end of the current 30-min window: kWh so far scaled up by the share of the book that has reported (kWh ÷ meter coverage).

panels.title: Active Meters
panels.description: Distinct meters that reported at least one reading in the current window (HyperLogLog ±1%). Book size ≈ 5,083 meters.

panels.title: Duplicates (5 min)
panels.description: Payloads skipped by Redis dedup in the last 5 minutes. >3% may indicate vendor chaos mode.

panels.title: Invalid Readings (5 min)
panels.description: Readings rejected by validation (null kWh, out-of-range) in the last 5 minutes.

panels.title: Last Delivery Accepted
panels.description: Wall-clock time the most recent delivery was accepted into batch_log (live + backfill). Answers BRIEF feed-health: 'when was the last delivery accepted?'. NOT filtered by the dashboard's 2013-14 data-time range — logged_at is real receipt time.

targets.rawSql: SELECT MAX(logged_at) AS "Last delivery" FROM batch_log

panels.title: Deliveries — 5 min vs Baseline
panels.description: Left: deliveries accepted in the last 5 minutes. Right: the trailing hour's average per 5-min bucket (hour count / 12). A last-5-min value well below baseline means the feed is slowing. Answers BRIEF feed-health: 'how many deliveries in the last 5 minutes vs the trailing hour's baseline?'.

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE logged_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '5 minutes') AS "Last 5 min", ROUND(COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE logged_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 hour')::numeric / 12.0, 1) AS "Baseline /5 min" FROM batch_log

panels.title: kWh per 30-min Settlement Window — Last 24 h
panels.description: Total kWh consumed by all 12,000 homes per window. Source: clean_readings (PostgreSQL).

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('minute', reading_time) - (EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM reading_time)::int % 30) * INTERVAL '1 minute' AS time, SUM(kwh) AS total_kwh FROM clean_readings WHERE $__timeFilter(reading_time) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Rejection Rate by Type — Last 24 h
panels.description: Hourly count of rejected readings, split by rejection reason, bucketed by reading_time (data-time). Spikes indicate vendor data quality issues.

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('hour', reading_time) AS time, rejection_type AS metric, COUNT(*) AS value FROM rejected_readings WHERE reading_time IS NOT NULL AND $__timeFilter(reading_time) GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Silent Meters — Last Detection
panels.description: Meters that have not reported in the last 2+ hours, as detected by the scheduler. Updated every 30 min.

targets.rawSql: SELECT meter_id, postcode_area, last_seen, ROUND(silent_hours::numeric, 1) AS silent_hours, detected_at FROM meter_silence_log ORDER BY silent_hours DESC LIMIT 50
